symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden symmetrical acral eruption spreading centripetally from hands/wrists to the trunk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acral-predominant symmetrical onset with centripetal spread is the characteristic distribution of erythema multiforme.

symptoms.symptom_name: Classic three-zone target/iris lesions with a dusky/blistered centre, pale ring and erythematous halo

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The true three-zone target lesion with a fixed central epidermal-damage zone is near-defining for erythema multiforme and distinguishes it from urticaria and other annular eruptions.
Urticarial wheals are transient/migratory and blanching without a fixed dusky/blistered epidermal centre.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding herpes labialis (cold sore) ~10 days before the eruption

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: HSV is the single commonest trigger of erythema multiforme, classically 1-2 weeks after a cold-sore outbreak — the temporal link strongly supports the diagnosis and its benign minor variant.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new medication in the preceding weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a new drug and the presence of an HSV trigger argue against a drug-triggered fixed drug eruption or a drug-triggered SJS/TEN.
SJS/TEN is most often drug-triggered; the absence of a new culprit drug lowers (but does not eliminate) that concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Burning/itch predominant over pain, systemically well and anxious

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild burning/itch with preserved wellbeing fits the benign minor variant; prominent skin pain would instead raise concern for the severe (SJS/TEN) end of the spectrum.

symptoms.symptom_name: No mucosal (mouth/eye/genital) erosions, no skin sloughing, no severe skin pain, no high fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of mucosal erosions, skin detachment, skin pain and systemic upset argues strongly against SJS/TEN and defines the benign MINOR variant — though the blistering morphology mandates keeping it as a safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: No facial/lip/tongue/throat swelling, no breathing difficulty, no widespread urticaria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of mucocutaneous swelling, airway symptoms and systemic urticaria makes an evolving systemic allergic reaction unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician mucosal examination showing no oral/conjunctival/genital erosions, negative Nikolsky sign, no skin sloughing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person examination: absent mucosal erosions and a negative Nikolsky sign confirm the minor variant and exclude the severe mucocutaneous end of the spectrum. Cannot be performed over telehealth — the basis for the mandatory SJS/TEN safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 125/80, HR 88, Temp 37.4, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 76 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement; near-normal, essentially afebrile and haemodynamically stable vitals argue against a severe systemic reaction. Not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Review (in person or video) of the skin lesions and the oral/ocular/genital mucosae to confirm erythema multiforme minor and exclude SJS/TEN
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct inspection of the target lesions and, crucially, the mucous membranes and skin integrity (Nikolsky sign) is the assessment telehealth cannot do and is what confirms the benign minor variant.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Consideration of HSV suppressive therapy assessment if episodes become recurrent
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For frequently recurrent HSV-associated erythema multiforme, elective review for prophylactic antiviral suppression can prevent recurrences; not required for this single acute episode.
Not indicated for a classic clinically-evident erythema multiforme minor with a clear HSV trigger; reserved for diagnostic uncertainty or atypical/severe presentations.
No systemic features — routine acute bloods would be over-investigation of a localised, self-limiting condition; reserve for suspected SJS/TEN or systemic upset.
